name: Polie with the 'must be Bogey or Better' gate ON — proves the GROSS gate VOIDS an ineligible polie and that this CHANGES the ledger (vs gate OFF on identical inputs). All nets = par (base 0); gross set per the gate.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the F1 polie modifier (Story 2.3), polieBogeyOrBetter:true. A checked polie counts only if that player's GROSS is a finite number ≤ par+1 (bogey-or-better); a non-finite or worse-than-bogey gross VOIDS the polie. The gate is on GROSS (raw strokes) — never net — so the group's 'net off the low' basis cannot affect it. The golden test ALSO recomputes these SAME inputs with polieBogeyOrBetter:false and asserts a DIFFERENT ledger, proving the gate moves money. SettlementEdge IR = post-2.1a whole-dollar 1-to-1.
_handCalc:
Teams A={a1,a2} B={b1,b2}, flat $5/pt, polie ON with bogey-or-better gate ON. Every net = par => base 0. Gross gates each checked polie (gross ≤ par+1):
  H1 (par4): a1 polie, a1 gross=4 (=par, ≤5) => ELIGIBLE => +1.
  H2 (par5): a2 polie, a2 gross=6 (=bogey=par+1, ≤6) => ELIGIBLE => +1.
  H3 (par4): b1 polie, b1 gross=6 (=double=par+2, >5) => VOIDED => 0.
Round polieA (gate ON) = +1+1+0 = +2 => a1=a2=+1000c (+$10), b1=b2=−1000c. Edges b1->a1 1000, b2->a2 1000. total 2000c.
GATE MOVES MONEY: the same inputs with polieBogeyOrBetter:false give H3 b1 => −1 => polieA=+1 => a1=a2=+500c, total 1000c. The golden test asserts BOTH (toggle ON = $10/side, OFF = $5/side); B's voided double-bogey polie is the difference.
